zh.lifeSummary: 當Margaret le Boteler於大約1278年出生在英國藍卡爾貝福 霍爾時，她的父親Sir Henry le Boteler - Lord of Warrington, MP25歲，母親Isabel le Boteler25歲。她去世於大約1325年在英國爾瓦布爾頓享年49歲。

nameMeanings.name: Boteler
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: occupational name for a maker of leather bottles, from an agent derivative of Middle English botel, Old French bouteille ‘bottle’.

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

nameMeanings.name: le
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of   Lewis   or respelling of   Lou  , as borne by the impresario  Lew Grade  (  1906–98 , originally  Louis Winogradsky ).

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

en.lifeSummary: When Margaret le Boteler was born about 1278, in Bewsey Hall, Lancashire, England, her father, Sir Henry le Boteler - Lord of Warrington, MP, was 25 and her mother, Isabel le Boteler, was 25. She died about 1325, in Warburton, Cheshire, England, at the age of 49.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Margaret le Boteler est née environ 1278, à Bewsey Hall, Lancashire, Angleterre, son père, Sir Henry le Boteler - Lord of Warrington, MP, avait 25 ans et sa mère, Isabel le Boteler, avait 25 ans. Elle est décédée environ 1325, à Warburton, Cheshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 49 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Margaret le Boteler um 1278 in Bewsey Hall, Lancashire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Sir Henry le Boteler - Lord of Warrington, MP 25 und ihre Mutter Isabel le Boteler 25. Sie starb um 1325 im Alter von 49 Jahren in Warburton, Cheshire, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Margaret le Boteler è nata il circa 1278 nel Bewsey Hall, Lancashire, England, sua padre, Sir Henry le Boteler - Lord of Warrington, MP, aveva 25 anni e sua madre, Isabel le Boteler, aveva 25 anni. Morì circa 1325, nel Warburton, Cheshire, England, all'età di 49 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: Margaret le Boteler(이)가 대략 1278년 영국 랭카실르 브w시 홀에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir Henry le Boteler - Lord of Warrington, MP(은)는 25세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Isabel le Boteler(은)는 25세였다. 그녀는 대략 1325년 영국 체실르 워버턴에서 49세의 나이로 사망했다.
